text:
Isaac (イサク, Isaku) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 202) was a Greed Island player, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 130 hired by Battera, and a founding member of an alliance formed to clear the game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 

Appearance

Isaac had hair neatly combed and parted to the right, a thin mustache and eyebrows, and a Grecian nose. He wore a sleeveless tunic with linear markings around the collar and one around the lower waist area, a long-sleeved shirt, pants, and shoes. 

Personality

Like his fellow alliance members, he had an informative personality with extensive knowledge on the game. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Alongside Genthru, Nomdieu, Assam, and Nickes, Isaac tries to recruit new players Abengane, Puhat, Biscuit, Gon, and Killua to their alliance. During their pitch, Isaac tells them that they are the last group of potential recruits and assures them that they can collect all the cards in a month once things start moving. He also lets them know that they have a secure place to store the cards they collect. The group manages to convince Puhat and Abengane to join, but fail to convince Gon, Killua, and Biscuit to join for their own respective reasons. 

Although supposed founding members of the alliance, he and Nomdieu were left out of a conversation between Genthru, Assam, and Nickes. It's uncertain whether Isaac was at the alliance base when Genthru revealed himself as the Bomber. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 Along with the rest of the alliance (aside from Abengane and Cuzco), he dies after the bombs set by Genthru are detonated simultaneously. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 

Later, Isaac's name is mentioned in Gon's binder when the latter looks to see which players are dead or alive. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Isaac also had none. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Isaac was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Isaac's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 

Anime and Manga Differences

Isaac does not appear in the 1999 anime adaptation.
